summary: Possible memory leak in Replset Topology Monitor
description:
We've been debugging memory leak issues in our node application using LLDB and noticed a large number of Timeout classes sitting in memory. Digging into it,w e found there were over 1000 timeouts (which had already executed) attached to the ReplSet, as you can see in the output below (from just a few hours of running).

0x00003b5ae4974ec1:<Object: ReplSet properties {
    .domain=0x0000084bfe804201:<null>,
    ._events=0x00003b5ae4985041:<Object: EventHandlers>,
    ._eventsCount=<Smi: 19>,
    ._maxListeners=0x0000084bfe804311:<undefined>,
    .id=<Smi: 1>,
    .s=0x00003b5ae4985059:<Object: Object>,
    .authProviders=0x00003b5ae49796b1:<Object: Object>,
    .initialConnectState=0x00003b5ae49850e9:<Object: Object>,
    .state=0x00002ba102135261:<String: "connected">,
    .haTimeoutId=0x0000084bfe804201:<null>,
    .authenticating=0x0000084bfe804421:<false>,
    .ismaster=0x00003b5ae4985119:<Object: Object>,
    .intervalIds=0x00003b5ae4985151:<Array: length=1083>}>

Looking at the code in mongodb-core/lib/topologies/replset.js and the toggleMonitor function, you see timeouts being pushed onto the intervalIds array (which you can see has 1083 timeouts on it in the LLD output). However, I don't see anywhere that these timeouts are being removed from the array once they fire.

summary: cursor.count ignores hint
HA monitoring does not cover new replica set members
Provide the Ability to get the first topologyDescriptionChanged event.
Add hasNext() for command and aggregation cursors
description:
Unlike with regular [{{Cursor}}|http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/2.2/api/Cursor.html], [{{AggregationCursor}}|http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/2.2/api/AggregationCursor.html] and [{{CommandCursor}}|http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/2.2/api/CommandCursor.html] do not have the {{hasNext()}} method.

With {{hasNext()}} missing it's not possible to work with these cursors in exactly the same fashion as with regular {{Cursor}}.

summary: Callback functions weren't be called when service mongod restart
description:
We're upgrading MongoDB NodeJS Driver from version 1.4.8 to 2.2.27
We did load testing to check if the our functionalities are still working.

We found that there are some callback functions weren't be called when we did restart mongod. I write code snippet below which can reproduce the issue.

The code connect to replica set which composite of 3 servers: dtci-ctamongo-01, dtci-ctamongo-02 and dtci-ctamongo-03.
dtci-ctamongo-01 is primary server.

While the code below was running, I did run `sudo service mongod restart` command on primary server.
After that some callback functions were called with error. But that's not all. There were still some waiting to be called.

CODE:
{code:javascript}
var MongoClient = require('mongodb').MongoClient;
var cnxString = 'mongodb://dtci-ctamongo-01:27017,dtci-ctamongo-02:27017,dtci-ctamongo-03:27017/etap?replicaSet=etap';

MongoClient.connect(cnxString, function (error, db) {
	if(error) return console.log('connect error:', error);
	var i = 0, j = 0;
	for(; i < 100000; i++) {
		db.listCollections().toArray(function(error, items) {
			j++;
			if(error) console.log('callback error:', j, error.message);
			else console.log('callback ok: ', j);
		});
	}
	console.log('' + i, "commands sent to driver");
});
{code}

OUTPUT:
{noformat}
100000 commands sent to driver
callback ok:  1
callback ok:  2
callback ok:  3

summary: Add guard condition to GridFS to ward of race condition where CheckDone could be called twice
Fix GridStore issue caused by Node 8.0.0 breaking backward compatible fs.read API
Replace .skip in download.js for gridfs and replace with $gte query to ensure index usage
Use actual server type in standalone SDAM events:
description:
Use actual server type in standalone SDAM events:
Currently the server type inside the topologyDescriptionChanged event
for a single topology is 'Standalone', which is unexpected. According to
the SDAM specification a TopologyDescription has a list of
ServerDescriptions, which had a type field indicating the type of server
it is and does not state that the server type never changes if the
topology is single.

My expectation is that a single connection to a replica set primary, for
example, would give me a topology type of 'Single' and a server type of
'RSPrimary', not 'Standalone' like it currently does.

The serverDescriptionChanged event properly has the type set.

I could not get the tests running locally - they just stop after the first step `[purge the directories]` - hence I have not added tests but the existing sdam server test should still pass.

summary: cursor.nextObject doesn't return promise?
Remove `Cursor.nextObject` and document changes
description: `Cursor.nextObject` has been deprecated for some time, and should be removed in the upcoming semver major release. We need to remove it, as well as any tests related to it, and then update all documentation using (or defining) that method.

summary: secondaryPreferred reads from all nodes
Add .replaceRoot to AggregationCursor
description:
MongoDB 3.4+ supports a $replaceRoot stage while aggregating: https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/operator/aggregation/replaceRoot/#pipe._S_replaceRoot

`lib/aggregation_cursor.js` seems to define prototype / class methods for method chaining instead of using an array/object-based raw pipeline. `.replaceRoot({ newRoot: '$xyz' })` isn't available there yet and I'd like to add it to make our code (which uses chaining elsewhere) consistent.

I would be happy to submit a pull request for this, the only thing I was unsure about was how to limit the command to servers of version 3.4+ only, and whether this is necessary. Can someone advise me on this?

summary: Have TCP keepalive default to true
updates scram.js hi() algorithm to utilize crypto.pbkdf2Sync()
description:
From github pull request https://github.com/christkv/mongodb-core/pull/184 .

This pull request fixes a few different issues.

The previous hi() algorithm was basically a manually written version of crypto.pbkdf2Sync(). My guess is that this library predates the addition of that method in Node v0.9.3. That method in crypto is a C++ native algorithm so it's able to perform the computations dramatically faster. In example on my machine, the previous hi() algorithm would take around 200-300ms while the same pbkdf2Sync version only takes around 12ms.

The hi() function is deterministic in the sense that for the same arguments you get the same result back, always. The reason this matter is that when using hi() each worker in the pool will call hi() with the exact same arguments. That means it's doing the same computationally intense call for each worker in the pool even though all of them will utilize the same resulting string. In this fork I'm storing the result of the previous call so that way it only performs the computation once per boot.

After the clientFinal generation it appears there was some left-behind code. // Generate server key and // Generate server signature. Neither of those calls appears to have their result used anywhere in the rest of the function. Removing them appears to have no regression.

If you have questions let me know. Thanks.

summary: Fix .cursor() method documentation in Core Server, Replset and Mongos
description:
The docs for mongodb-core for the classes {{Server}}, {{Replset}} and {{Mongos}} list the arguments for the {{.cursor()}} method as {{ns, cmd, callback}}. This is incorrect: the third argument is an {{options}} object.

This method returns a cursor. The docs currently do not say what this method returns.

Link to the relevant methods in the docs:
* http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/core/api/Server.html#cursor
* http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/core/api/ReplSet.html#cursor
* http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/core/api/Mongos.html#cursor

summary: Cannot projection field name is 'partial'
Create MongoNetworkError and update relevant error handlers
description: Create a new {{MongoNetworkError}} class specifically for network-related errors. {{MongoNetworkError}} will extend {{MongoError}}. Then go through update relevant error handlers of network-related errors to use {{MongoNetworkError}} instead of {{MongoError}}. This will facilitate determining whether an error is "resumable" for the purpose of Change Streams (NODE-1057).

summary: initializeUnorderedBulkOp should not reject Promise on writeErrors
description:
When doing a initializeUnorderedBulkOp, and (at least) one write operation fails, if I pass a callback (err, result), I get both the error and the result, where the error is pretty much useless, but in the result I can get the WriteErrors explaining what failed.

But when I don't pass a callback and get a promise back, the promise gets rejected, and all I get is a useless error in the .catch, but I have no way of getting the full WriteErrors.

I believe the Promise returned should be rejected only when the whole operation fails (eg. connection error) but if one of the many operations in the bulk fails, it should still resolve the promise, and the developer should manage exceptions.

summary: Metamocha does not support Mocha options
description: When [using Mocha programatically|https://github.com/mochajs/mocha/wiki/Using-mocha-programmatically], the docs specify that you can pass in a list of options when creating your Mocha instance. Currently, Metamocha does not allow for this, and we would like to add it in to make Metamocha as flexible as Mocha is.

summary: Metamocha doesn't filter tests correctly
description: Currently, Metamocha applies every filter to every test, and if the test passes one filter, then it's included in the run. Also, filters assume that every test has metadata relevant to that specific filter, and we would rather have filters skip tests that don't have relevant metadata

summary: Remove Change Stream Db support
description: Change Streams no longer support being created on databases. The currently-written code for this needs to be stripped out and eventually placed into a new PR.

summary: Fix .skip and .only in Metamocha
Switch mongodb-test-runner to not depend on npm's version of mongodb-core
description: We want the test runner to depend on the local version of {{mongodb-core}}, rather than the version available on npm. To do this, we need to recursively traverse up the dependency tree to find the version of {{mongodb-core}} that uses {{mongodb-test-runner}} as a dependency.

summary: Support full Node streaming interface with resumability
Use MockupDB to trigger MongoNetworkErrors
description: At the moment we are using a hacky {{SIGSTOP}} command to trigger timeouts. This is error-prone. We should use mockupdb instead.

summary: Cursor.prototype.hasNext does not propagate errors when using callbacks
description: If you pass a callback to `Cursor.prototype.hasNext`, and some internal error occurs, it will not be passed up back to the user.

summary: TypeError: callback is not a function
description:
I get very random behaviors from the driver when executing a lot of conccurent queries simultaniously.
When executing a batch of ~1000 parallel queries, I get two different behaviours:
  - The task succeeds (~5% of the time)
  - I get the following error (with longjohn enabled):

{code:javascript}
/home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb/lib/utils.js:120
      return value2 ? callback(err, value1, value2) :  callback(err, value1);
                                                       ^
TypeError: callback is not a function
    at /home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb/lib/cursor.js:779:15
    at handleCallback (/home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb/lib/utils.js:120:56)
    at /home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb/lib/cursor.js:745:22
    at handleCallback (/home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb/lib/utils.js:120:56)
    at /home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb/lib/cursor.js:681:20
    at nextFunction (/home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/cursor.js:521:12)
    at /home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/cursor.js:593:7
    at queryCallback (/home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/cursor.js:232:18)
    at handleOperationCallback (/home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/connection/pool.js:474:7)
    at /home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/connection/pool.js:504:9
---------------------------------------------
    at handleCallback (/home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb/lib/utils.js:123:13)
    at /home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb/lib/cursor.js:745:22
    at handleCallback (/home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb/lib/utils.js:120:56)
    at /home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb/lib/cursor.js:681:20
    at nextFunction (/home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/cursor.js:521:12)
    at /home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/cursor.js:593:7
    at queryCallback (/home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/cursor.js:232:18)
    at handleOperationCallback (/home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/connection/pool.js:474:7)
    at /home/admin/parser/attempt/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/connection/pool.js:504:9
admin@ip-172-33-1-249:~/parser/attempt$ vi node_modules/mongodb/lib/utils.js
{code}

The task consists of a Promise.all([]) containing async function calls containing awaited queries (using async/await keywords)... So I am not using any callbacks, only promises.
I am quite confused as the same task sometimes succeeds and sometime fails for no apparent reason.

Because of the error message, I have tried wrapping the line triggering the error with a if (typeof callback === 'function') but I still get the same error!!

{code:javascript}
    if(callback && typeof callback === 'function') {
      return value2 ? callback(err, value1, value2) :  callback(err, value1);
    }
{code}

Server-side, the last message I have is the following:

{code}
[conn1225] AssertionException handling request, closing client connection: 6 socket exception [SEND_ERROR] for 127.0.0.1:34316
{code}

Note that if I separate my big 1000 queries call into smaller batches, it fixes the issue.

summary: the result of db.dropDatabase is "true" but the database and it's contents still exist when I check from the terminal.
add support for { nameOnly: true } to `listDatabases`
description: Right now the `listDatabases` command returns a list of the databases on a server. In Mongo 3.2.13 they added the capability to return only the database names, without the stats. This allows it to not need locks in order to return the data.

summary: updateOne and updateMany must contain at least one atomic operator
Aggregation commands are required to have a `cursor` key
description:
The CRUD spec specifies: 
{code}
/**
   * Indicates whether the command will request that the server provide results using a cursor.
   *
   * If true, this option is sent with a value of "cursor: {}". The default value is true.
   * For servers < 2.6, this option is ignored and not sent as aggregation cursors are not available.
   *
   * @see https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/command/aggregate/
   */
  useCursor: Optional<Boolean>;
{code}

The node driver does not follow this behavior, and it's become a problem because support was deprecated in version 3.4 of the server, and fully removed in 3.6.

This will have to be fixed for both the 2.x and 3.x branches of the node driver, with one behavioral change:
  - 2.x will continue to massage the resulting cursor into the expected array form, so as not to break existing applications
  - 3.x will simply always return an aggregation cursor

summary: clean mocha test output
description: Presently, switching our test suite over to mocha has exposed a number of cases where random debug output or deprecation warnings are displayed. This is highly distracting when trying to see errors, and should be cleaned up

summary: refactor mock server to track individual mock servers to reduce flakiness
description: Mock servers must be manually and explicitly destroyed at the end of each test. Now that we use mocha, we can utilize test hooks in order to manage our state more effectively.  Not only does this mean we can centralize where mock servers are cleaned up (in the mock module itself), but we can also ensure that when one test fails it does not bring down the rest of the batch (because they tend to use the same port numbers).

summary: js-bson version 2.0.0 - drop node < 4 support
description:
update to use the same travis.yaml file from mongodb-native (3.0.0 branch) 

update package.json (engines?) with supported node versions

summary: js-bson is not spec-compliant
description: For one, js-bson does not support the "x" regex option. Need to run tests and see where else updates are needed.

summary: Reduce duplication in timestamp type in js-bson by using long
Cache SCRAM-SHA-1 ClientKey
